description: Documentation and proof checklist for Chinese warehouse automation vendors preparing a first Italian pilot.

items.name: Technical specifications
items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: Operations and engineering teams need operating limits before allowing equipment into a warehouse pilot.

items.name: CE and safety documentation summary
items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: HSE and operations teams need a buyer-readable summary of safety evidence before pilot approval.

items.name: Pilot-area risk assessment method
items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: Italian warehouses need a practical way to review mixed traffic, operator interaction, and emergency procedures.

items.name: Installation and commissioning plan
items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: The buyer must know how installation avoids disrupting dispatch, production, or client SLA windows.

items.name: Maintenance plan
items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: Warehouse managers need confidence that the system can remain available during daily operations.

items.name: Pilot support SLA
items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: The buyer needs defined response expectations if the pilot affects live warehouse work.

items.name: Spare parts availability plan
items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: The buyer needs assurance that small failures will not stop the pilot for weeks.

items.name: WMS workflow integration note
items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: Operations and IT need to know how tasks move between the warehouse system and the automation layer.

items.name: API or data exchange summary
items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: IT teams need a clear view of interfaces before approving even a lightweight pilot.

items.name: Data security and access summary
items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: IT and compliance stakeholders need to understand operational data access, storage, and retention during the pilot.

items.name: Italy-localized ROI model
items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: The buyer needs a business case tied to its own labor, throughput, walking time, error, or manual-handling baseline.

items.name: Pilot KPI measurement plan
items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: A first pilot needs success criteria that operations and finance can review together.

items.name: Relevant case study evidence
items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: The buyer needs proof that the product has worked in a similar warehouse process, even if the first Italian reference does not exist yet.

items.name: Pilot exit conditions
items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: A clear exit path lowers procurement risk and makes a first pilot easier to approve.

items.name: Pilot warranty and responsibility terms
items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: The buyer needs to know who is responsible for faults, damage, replacement, and support during the pilot.

items.name: Operator and supervisor training pack
items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: Operators must understand how to work around or with the system safely and consistently.
minimum_content: Safety do and do not list

items.name: Throughput test evidence
items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: Sorting, picking, and transport buyers need evidence that the system can handle realistic Italian warehouse volumes.

items.name: Validation support note for regulated warehouses
items.why_italian_buyer_needs_it: Pharma and healthcare logistics buyers need to understand how the vendor supports quality review and change control.
